concepts.title: Definition of E-waste
concepts.description: E-waste refers to electronic waste, which includes discarded electrical and electronic equipment such as computers, smartphones, televisions, and other electronic devices.

concepts.title: Effects of E-waste
concepts.description: The improper disposal of e-waste can lead to environmental pollution, health problems, and the depletion of natural resources.

concepts.title: Types of E-waste
concepts.description: E-waste can be broadly categorized into two types: B2C (Business to Consumer) and B2B (Business to Business). B2C e-waste includes household appliances and consumer electronics, while B2B e-waste includes industrial and commercial electronics.

concepts.title: E-waste Disposal Hierarchy
concepts.description: The 3Rs of e-waste management are Reduce, Reuse, and Recycle. It can be remembered using the acronym R3.

concepts.title: Incorrect E-waste Disposal Methods
concepts.description: Students often mistake incineration and open burning of e-waste as acceptable disposal methods. However, these methods can lead to air and water pollution. The correct methods include recycling and safe disposal through authorized e-waste collection centers.

concepts.title: E-waste Generation Rate
concepts.description: The global e-waste generation rate is approximately 50 million metric tons annually, with a projected increase to 74 million metric tons by 2030.

concepts.title: E-waste Recycling Methods
concepts.description: E-waste can be recycled through various methods, including mechanical recycling, chemical recycling, and material recovery facilities (MRFs).

concepts.title: Role of Government in E-waste Management
concepts.description: The government plays a crucial role in e-waste management through legislation, regulations, and awareness campaigns. It can encourage responsible e-waste disposal practices and provide incentives for e-waste recycling.
